question.text: In the Hemant Ghai case, what was ordered regarding the open positions in derivatives contracts held by the restrained entities?

answer.text: They could close out/square up till 3 months from date of order
They had to be immediately liquidated
They were automatically cancelled by the exchange
They could be held until expiry

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The order stated that any open position in derivatives contract could be closed out/squared up till 3 months from the date of the order. **The Trap:** Immediate liquidation would cause market disruption. Holding until expiry would defeat the purpose of the restraint order. **Cross-Exam Context:** Treatment of existing derivative positions during restraint orders is tested in NISM Series III-A and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 3-month window balances enforcement objectives with practical considerations of unwinding derivative positions without excessive market impact.
